observations.recommendation: Municipal Accountant expedites the retrieval of documents pertaining to the amortization of the Municipality’s outstanding loans and coordinate with the DBP for the reconciliation of records.
observations.reason: The DBP has already furnished the Municipal Accountant with the copy of documents detailing the payment records of the LGU on the loan. However, reconciliation has yet to be completed.

observations.recommendation:
Management exerts all efforts to retrieve the necessary documents supporting the releases and payments of Loans Receivable-Domestic to establish the validity and the accuracy of the reported balance in the books of accounts.

Management exercises its power to collect the loaned amount from the debtors, as may be warranted.

MBO revisits and follows the prescribed process of budget augmentation;

ensures that appropriations per Annual Budget and SAAO are reconciled and submit complete supporting documents to facilitate reconciliation of any difference.
observations.reason: There are still noted unreconciled differences in the SAAOB.

observations.recommendation: Municipal Treasurer coordinates with the PHIC to determine the true nature of the noted deficiencies, reconciles records of both agencies, and undertakes all the necessary subsequent steps to settle the deficiencies, as may be warranted.
observations.reason: The latest Statement of Premium Account from PHIC as of March 2025 still shows deficiencies.

observations.recommendation:
Municipal Treasurer observes prompt submission of documents to the Municipal Accountant to ensure that transactions are recorded in the journals and posted in the general ledger on time.

Municipal Accountant ensures that general ledgers are always updated and monthly BRS prepared on time to ensure accuracy of the balances of affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.reason:
There is still delay in the transmittal of documents from the Treasury to the Accounting. This is reiterated in AO No. 1, Part II of this report.

The Municipal Accountant has only prepared the bank reconciliation statements for 6 out of the 15 bank accounts. This is reiterated in AO No. 1, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant conduct reconciliation of their records at least quarterly consistent with Section 6.3, of COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.reason: No reconciliation was made between the DO’s cashbook and the Accounting’s book of accounts.

observations.recommendation: LDRRM Officer prepares and submits the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the COA on or before the 15th day after the end of each month consistent with Paragraph 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.reason: The LDRRMO did not submit the Report on Utilization of DRRM Fund monthly.

The Municipal Treasurer did not prepare the PCs.

The Municipal Accountant did not prepare the PPELC, RPLC, and CIPLC.

The inventory committee did not conduct the physical inventory of all properties.

observations.recommendation: Management, in coordination with offices concerned, revisits COA Circular No. 2020-006 and complies with all the guidelines and procedures provided therein for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts.
observations.reason: There was no substantial compliance of the guidelines and procedures for one-time cleansing by the management.

observations.recommendation: Municipal Treasurer enforces the remittance of collections of the revenue collectors to the liquidating officer daily as required under Section 29 of the New Government Accounting System Volume I to prevent the accumulation of funds in the hands of the collectors/tellers.
observations.reason: This is for further monitoring.

observations.recommendation: Municipal Treasurer deposits all collections intact daily or the next banking day with the authorized depository bank in accordance with Section 32 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Vol. 1
observations.reason: This is for further monitoring.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to require the following:

the Municipal Accountant to ensure that ledgers are properly maintained and regularly updated, while also promptly coordinating with the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure accurate recording of fund disbursements;

the MBO to ensure that all disbursements are valid charges to the fund and are supported by corresponding appropriations; and

the Municipal Treasurer to ensure the availability of funds to fully support the expenditures and obligations charged to the fund.
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure that appropriate accounts are used in recording the transactions to reflect the balances of the accounts to their accurate amounts.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to make the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the affected PPEs to inventory/expense and to correct the balances of the affected accounts. Henceforth, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to record the tangible items costing below ₱50,000.00 under semi-expendable asset account upon purchase, and under semi-expendable expense account upon issuance.

We recommended and management agreed to make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balances of the affected accounts. Henceforth, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure that expenses are recorded on the period in which they are incurred in accordance with the accrual basis of accounting for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and the HRMO agreed to recompute the deficiencies in the shares of the employees and the government in the employees’ Pag-IBIG contributions, and make the necessary arrangements in order to withhold and remit the deficiencies to the Pag-IBIG Fund. Further, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to make the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts in order to correct the balances of the affected accounts.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to recognize withholding tax upon recording the liability at year-end by crediting Due to BIR and recording Accounts Payable at net of tax to minimize the risk of administrative penalties and ensure the accurate presentation of financial statements.

We recommended and the MBO agreed to submit a written justification as to the charging of the payment for CY 2023 expenses against the CY 2024 budget. Further, we recommended and the MBO agreed to ensure that disbursements/expenses are properly charged against the year of incurrence; and the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure that expenses are recorded in the year in which they are incurred consonant to the accrual basis of accounting.

We recommended and management agreed to discontinue the practice of staggered procurement of office supplies and to direct the BAC Chairman and the respective end-users of various offices to meticulously plan their supply requirements. This will ensure compliance with the relevant provisions of the IRR of RA 9184 and promote a more efficient and cost-effective procurement process.

We recommended and the management agreed to send demand letters to the concerned municipal officers and employees to enforce immediate settlement of the outstanding cash advances and make a proper accounting or liquidation thereof.

We recommended and management agreed to ensure that full liquidation of CAs is rendered first by the accountable officers before new ones are granted for stronger internal control providing safeguards on the disbursement of Municipal funds.
We recommended and management agreed to request approval from the Executive Director of the BLGF to transact and avail services of a private bank in compliance with DOF DC No. 02-2022.

We recommended and management agreed to ensure that all municipal vehicles are marked “For Official Use Only” and bear government plates in compliance with COA Circular No. 77-61. Further, we recommended and management agreed to address the causes of non-renewal of the three (3) municipal vehicles’ LTO registration and have the registration renewed afterwards. Furthermore, coordinate with the PCSO for the execution of the Deed of Donation in order to ensure ownership of the donated Hiace 2.8 Commuter Deluxe.

We recommended and the Municipal Engineer agreed to strictly follow the guidelines outlined in DPWH DO No. 197, series of 2016, when preparing and computing the ABC for infrastructure projects to prevent overestimated costs.

We recommended and management agreed to require contractors of the aforementioned projects to post a retention bond and warranty security, for projects still within the defects liability period. Additionally, we recommended and management agreed to issue the Certificate of Final Acceptance upon verification that no claims have been filed against them.

We recommended and management agreed to prioritize filling vacant positions by hiring new employees or expediting the promotion of deserving staff to enhance services, ensure the effective implementation of LGU programs and activities, maximize benefits for constituents, and promote equal job opportunities within the community.

We recommended and the HRMO agreed to require the workers hired on a “job order” status to regularly prepare accomplishment reports and submit the same, together with the other required documents under Item 1.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001, in support of their claims for wages.

We recommended and management agreed to require the LDRRMC to revise the LDRRMFIP following the COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, by incorporating the detailed plan for the prior years’ unexpended balances recorded in the Trust Liabilities – LDRRM. Further, ensure that disbursements shall only be made out of the unexpended balance if included in the revised LDRRMFIP approved by the Sangguniang Bayan.
We recommended and management agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer to release RPT and CTC shares to the barangays within five (5) days after the end of each quarter.
We recommended and the management agreed to require LGU officials to support the disbursements for taxi fares with Official Receipts in accordance with Section 4(6) of the PD No. 1445.

We recommended and management agreed to ensure that transportation expenses covering vehicles that do not issue ORs, with amounts not exceeding ₱300, are supported with CENRR in order to substantiate the incurrence of expenses and to support the validity and correctness of amounts claimed.
Municipal Accountant prepares the necessary entries to correct the balances of the affected accounts as at December 31, 2023.

HRMO ensures that the LGU’s share in the contributions to the GSIS Social Insurance Fund, Pag-IBIG Fund, and National Health Insurance Fund are duly computed and related documents forwarded to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording.
Municipal Accountant prospectively recognizes the payments of passed-on GRT under Other Financial Charges to ensure fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

Municipal Accountant prepares the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balances of the affected accounts;

ensures that insurance premium payments are recorded initially under Prepaid Insurance and expense the same as incurred under Insurance Expense.

Municipal Accountant makes the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balances of the affected accounts;

ensures that transfers of unexpended balance of LDRRMF to the Trust Fund are recorded correctly in the books of accounts for fair presentation in the financial statements.
Disbursing Officer observes the proper maintenance of the check register by requiring the payees to acknowledge the receipt of checks and indicate the dates of such receipt.

Management instructs the LDRRMO to include projects, programs, and activities in the LDRRMFIP chargeable against prior years’ unexpended LDRRMF balances in order to maximize available funds to implement PPAs to enhance disaster preparedness and strengthen response capabilities.

Management enacts the appropriate ordinance for the creation of the LDRRMO and the three (3) mandatory positions to assist the Local DRRM Officer consistent with the pertinent provisions of RA No. 10121, its IRR and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1.

Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer submit the monthly pre-closing trial balance and SAAOB on the 20th day of the following month to facilitate the verification of transactions and reports of the LGU.

Municipal Agriculturist traces the record of these assets, regularly monitor the changes of Work/Zoo Animals and Breeding Stocks to facilitate the proper valuation of these assets in order to reflect an accurate value of the account.

Municipal Accountant evaluates the Receivable accounts and validates its existence and collectability;

effects appropriate measures in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 to process the writing-off of dormant accounts and consequently prevent the overstatement of the Receivable account in the year-end Financial Statements.
observations.reason: Closed due to other agency action. The Accounts Receivable amounting to ₱752,624.28 no longer appears in the schedule. The Loans Receivable totaling ₱930,000.00 is reiterated in another AOM. The Due from NGAs amounting to ₱4,127.50 represents accumulated small amounts from various payees related to election expenses, which should not have been recorded under this account. The Due from GOCCs amounting to ₱21,000.00 pertains to burial benefits paid to various payees, which also should not have been recorded under this account. The Due from LGUs amounting to ₱23,075.60 no longer appears in the schedule.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Offices of the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to conduct reconciliation of their records at least quarterly consistent with Section 6.3, of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that Management enact the appropriate ordinance for the creation of the LDRRMO and the three (3) mandatory positions to assist the Local DRRM Officer consistent with the pertinent provisions of RA No. 10121, its IRR and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management require the Local DRRM Officer to prepare and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the COA on or before the 15th day after the end of each month consistent with Paragraph 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare PCs for every class of PPE; and

the Municipal Accountant to prepare PPELC, RPLC and CIPLC, and facilitate reconciliation with the balances in the books of accounts, and PCs and RPCPPEs to be prepared by the MTO. 

Henceforth, require the inventory committee to immediately conduct the physical inventory of all properties and submit the corresponding RPCPPE to the auditor as required under existing regulations.

We recommended that Management, in coordination with offices concerned, revisit COA Circular No. 2020-006 and comply with all the guidelines and procedures provided therein for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer enforce the remittance of collections of the revenue collectors to the liquidating officer daily as required under Section 29 of the New Government Accounting System Volume I to prevent the accumulation of funds in the hands of the collectors/tellers.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to submit the monthly pre-closing trial balance and SAAOB on the 20th day of the following month to facilitate the verification of transactions and reports of the LGU.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Agriculturist to trace the record of these assets, regularly monitor the changes of Work/Zoo Animals and Breeding Stocks to facilitate the proper valuation of these assets in order to reflect an accurate value of the account.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant evaluate the Receivable accounts and validate its existence and collectability. 
 
Effect appropriate measures in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 to process the writing-off of dormant accounts and consequently prevent the overstatement of the Receivable account in the year-end Financial Statements.
We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary entries to correct the balances of the affected accounts as at December 31, 2023.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the HRMO to ensure that the LGU’s share in the contributions to the GSIS Social Insurance Fund, Pag-IBIG Fund, and National Health Insurance Fund are duly computed and related documents forwarded to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to expedite the retrieval of documents pertaining to the amortization of the Municipality’s outstanding loans and coordinate with the DBP for the reconciliation of records.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prospectively recognize the payments of passed-on GRT under Other Financial Charges to ensure fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balances of the affected accounts. Henceforth, ensure that insurance premium payments are recorded initially under Prepaid Insurance and expense the same as incurred under Insurance Expense.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balances of the affected accounts. Henceforth, ensure that transfers of unexpended balance of LDRRMF to the Trust Fund are recorded correctly in the books of accounts for fair presentation in the financial statements.
The appropriate adjustment was made through JEV No. 300-2024-01-00360 dated January 2, 2024 to correct the balances of Due to LGUs and Trust Liabilities-DRRMF.

We recommended and Management agreed to exert all efforts to retrieve the necessary documents supporting the releases and payments of Loans Receivable-Domestic to establish the validity and the accuracy of the reported balance in the books of accounts.
Further, we recommended and Management agreed to exercise its power to collect the loaned amount from the debtors, as may be warranted.

We recommended and the Disbursing Officer agreed to observe the proper maintenance of the check register by requiring the payees to acknowledge the receipt of checks and indicate the dates of such receipt.

We recommended and the MBO agreed to revisit and follow the prescribed process of budget augmentation. Further, we recommended that MBO ensure that appropriations per Annual Budget and SAAO are reconciled and submit complete supporting documents to facilitate reconciliation of any difference.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to coordinate with the PHIC to determine the true nature of the noted deficiencies, and reconcile records of both agencies. Further, undertake all the necessary subsequent steps to settle the deficiencies, as may be warranted.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to observe prompt submission of documents to the Municipal Accountant to ensure that transactions are recorded in the journals and posted in the general ledger on time. Further, we recommend that Municipal Accountant ensure that general ledgers are always updated and monthly BRS prepared on time to ensure accuracy of the balances of affected accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the LDRRMO to include projects, programs, and activities in the LDRRMFIP chargeable against prior years’ unexpended LDRRMF balances in order to maximize available funds to implement PPAs to enhance disaster preparedness and strengthen response capabilities.

We recommended that Management require the DO to liquidate time bound cash advances within the reglementary period and to refund any unutilized CA on time to mitigate the risk of loss or use of the CA other than the purpose for which it was intended, and to make the fund available for use by the Municipality for other purposes, consistent with Section 4.1.3 of the COA Circular No. 97-002.
We recommended that Management reevaluate its existing cash requirements and ensure compliance with the provisions of the fidelity bond requirement by increasing the amount of bond of the DO.

We recommended that Management require the DO to ensure that debit and credit columns in the Cashbook in recording transactions pertaining to Senior Citizens’ Fund are properly accomplished; and

The DO to consistently observe the monthly ruling and footing, and certification of balances in the Cashbooks to facilitate reconciliation of balances with subsidiary accounting records.

We further recommended that the DO indicate the dates of transactions in the Cashbook; and 

The Chief Accountant to indicate the true dates of liquidation of CAs in the books of accounts.

We recommended that Management require the MTO to prepare RCI for check disbursements and RD for cash disbursements, and submit the same to the Accounting Unit for proper recognition and monitoring consistent with the GAP and other pertinent Sections in the MNGAS Volume I.

We recommended that Management require the Local DRRM Officer to include the disbursements from the STF in the Report on Utilization of LDRRMF to adequately monitor the funds accumulated in the LDRRMF, its utilization, and balance as of a specific date, and to facilitate reconciliation with the Accounting Office.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant, MBO and Local DRRM Officer to coordinate with each other to establish the balance of the STF as at December 31, 2022 by determining the unexpended QRF and MF-MOOE for CYs 2018 to 2022; and 
 
Municipal Accountant to make the necessary entries in the GF and TF books. 

Henceforth, properly divide the MF between CO and MOOE, make an entry at year-end in both books for the unexpended MF-MOOE and QRF balances for the year, and revert prior years’ unexpended balances that remained unutilized after five (5) years to the general fund.

We recommended that Management require the Accounting Unit to ensure complete and proper preparation of JEVs by preparing JEVs for all transactions; indicating the proper account title in the “Accounts and Explanations”; and assigning JEV numbers sequentially and independently of the supporting documents; and 
 
Ensure proper recording and posting of transactions in the journals and ledgers by coordinating with the system developer to make the JEV numbers per actual JEV consistent with those in the journals and ledgers, and the posting the GL be done per transaction.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to re-examine all the SLs created for each account, make revisions in the SLs created taking into consideration the description of the accounts and SLs provided in the RCA, and document the revision adequately.

We recommended that Management require the HRMD to ensure that Job Order Forms are duly accomplished, acknowledged and signed when hiring JOs consistent with the pertinent CSC issuances to ascertain the fitness of the JOs in public service, and the obligations of both parties, and to facilitate review of the correctness of wages paid in relation thereto.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary accounting entries to bring the affected accounts to their correct balances. 
 
Henceforth, record the retention money as part of the cost of the asset by debiting appropriate asset account and crediting Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable.

We recommended that Management abide by the provisions of COA Circular No. 94-103 through the immediate utilization of the funds received, timely and proper liquidation of all implemented projects and remittance of the remaining balances thereof to source agencies or seek authority for the utilization of the excess funds.

Require the Municipal Accountant to effect the transfer of acquired assets booked in the Trust Fund to the General Fund books so that the required depreciation could be computed thereon so that the Building, Net Income and Government Equity accounts in the General Fund are accurately stated.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to follow the proper process of collection. 
 
Henceforth, deposit all collections intact daily or the next banking day with the authorized depository bank in accordance with Section 32 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Vol. 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer fully liquidate the Petty Cash Fund at the end of the year by returning/depositing the excess unused funds and preparing the necessary liquidation documents prescribed in the New Government Accounting Standards Volume 1 and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant in order for the latter to facilitate the preparation of the appropriate journal entries.

We recommended that Certification that the official/employee is not assigned with any government vehicle and a Certificate or evidence of service rendered in a month of an official/department head claiming RATA be submitted to serve as basis in computation of actual claims to comply with the above-mentioned regulations. The certification shall be issued by the Office of the HRMO for department heads covered under Section 7.1 of LBC No. 103 and by Sangguniang Secretary for those officials covered under Section 7.5 of the same Local Budget Circular.

We recommended that Management provide a written explanation about the non-posting in the GPPB online portal the documents relative to the procurement of projects undertaken under the Bayanihan to Heal as One Act. Also, communicate with the GPPB if delayed posting of Notice of Award is possible.

We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal Point System to institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-aggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation; and 

Undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing gender issues and ensure that the programs, projects and activities are responsive to the said issue.

We recommended that Management appropriate fund and resources for the revision/updating of the Local Revenue Code in accordance with Republic Act 7160 or the Local Government Code of 1991; and 

Coordinate with the Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG) and the Department of Finance (DOF) through the Bureau of Local Government Finance (BLGF) in the proper imposition of taxes and fees in accordance with Joint Circular No. 2019-01.

We recommend that management relieve the Municipal Treasurer of her disbursing function.  Instead, have the checks for financial assistance to qualified Senior Citizens and Person with Disabilities be in the name of the Disbursing Officer for proper recording and subsequent liquidation, and for an adequate internal control system.
observations.managementAction: Management made necessary arrangement with the Municipal Treasurer and Disbursing Officer will assume the above-mentioned responsibilities. In addition, committed that the disbursing officer shall make the proper recording and subsequent liquidation of the cash advances.
observations.reason: This was not implemented by management. This is reiterated in Part II.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the Municipal Agriculturist to trace the record of these assets, regularly monitor the changes of Work/Zoo Animals and Breeding Stocks to facilitate the proper valuation of these assets in order to reflect an accurate value of the account.
observations.managementAction: Management was not able to implement the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: Inherited balances. Reconciliation was said to be difficult.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant evaluate the Receivable accounts and validate its existence and collectability. Effect appropriate measures in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 to process the writing-off of dormant accounts and consequently prevent the overstatement of the Receivable account in the year-end Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Management was not able to implement the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: Minimal movement in CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant trace the Due to BIR, Due to GSIS, Due to Pag-ibig and Due to PhilHealth accounts and ascertain the validity of the balances of these payables. Effect the necessary adjustments relating to the accounts after reconciliation.
observations.managementAction: Management was not able to implement the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management appropriate fund and resources for the revision/updating of the Local Revenue Code in accordance with Republic Act 7160 or the Local Government Code of 1991 and coordinate with the Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG) and the Department of Finance (DOF) through the Bureau of Local Government Finance (BLGF) in the proper imposition of taxes and fees in accordance with Joint Circular No. 2019-01.
observations.managementAction: Management was not able to implement the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management exert extra effort to collect the loans extended to individual beneficiaries by sending demand letters and making follow-ups on the demand rendered.
observations.managementAction: Management was not able to implement the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: Minimal movement in CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Health Officer to practice the preparation of requisition issue slip and properly monitor the issuance of drugs and medicines in accordance with Section 41 of the New Government Accounting System Volume II.
observations.managementAction: Management was not able to implement the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: Inventory of medicines were made instead.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the purchases of welfare goods and supplies and materials using the perpetual inventory method pursuant to Section 114 of NGAS Volume I Manual and the Municipal Treasurer/GSO to prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and submit it to the Municipal Accountant which shall be used as basis in recording the issuances/expense account as required under Section 121 of the same manual.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management provide a written explanation on the recording process of the financial assistance. Also, we recommend that the Office of the Municipal Accountant reconcile the check issued with the distribution list from the Municipal Treasurer and set up any unreconciled amount as receivable. Forward a copy of the Liquidation Report and supporting documents to the COA Office for Audit.  

Further, instruct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the journal entries made as follows:

Dr. Advances to Special Disbursing Officer
Cr. Prior Year’s Adjustment
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to reconcile the charges made to the Bayanihan Grant to reflect the proper balance of the fund at year-end and provide accurate data for decision making.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit the supporting documents and photos specifying the detailed item specification and brand name of the rapid test kits purchased to prove compliance with FDA advisory.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason:
The following were submitted by management:

(1) Photos specifying the detailed item specification.
(2) Brand name of the rapid test kits purchased.
(3) Certificate of compliance with FDA advisory.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to capitalize the cost of major repair made on its firefighting equipment in accordance with IPSAS 17 on the Property, Plant and Equipment to reflect the accurate value of the account in the financial statement.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: The necessary adjusting entries were made.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management require the Municipal Accountant to record the fund transfers to its appropriate account as required in COA Circular No. 2015-009 in order to accurately present actual balances of the accounts in the year-end financial statements and facilitate monitoring of the implementation of the projects. 

Also, instruct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the journal entries as follows:

Dr. Due from Local Government Units
Cr. Prior Years Adjustments
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: Necessary adjusting entries were booked.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the Municipal Accountant to book the unrecorded assets and correct the accounting entries made to ensure the correctness of the account balances in the books at year-end.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: Necessary adjustment was made by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management provide a written explanation about the non-posting in the GPPB online portal the documents relative to the procurement of projects undertaken under the Bayanihan to Heal as One Act. Also, communicate with the GPPB if delayed posting of Notice of Award is possible.
observations.managementAction: The management already registered with GPPB online portal and submitted letter of explanation for the non-posting in the GPPB online portal.
observations.reason: The recommendation was partially implemented by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the GAD Focal Point System to: (i) institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-aggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation; and (ii) undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing gender issues and ensure that the programs, projects and activities are responsive to the said issue.
observations.managementAction: The management made necessary instruction with the focal person to strictly follow the recommendation.
observations.reason: The recommendation was partially implemented by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management increase the amount of bond of the DO or consider the use of ATM cards in the payment of salaries to its regular and elected employees to reduce the amount of cash in the hands of the DO and to safeguard government funds from possible loss or misuse in accordance with Sections 23 and 24 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: Already in talks with bank for atm arrangements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to trace the transactions recorded in the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts and make the necessary corrections/adjustments.  Moreover, require the Municipal Treasurer to settle these accounts to allow the creditor-fund to utilize the amounts for their intended purposes, if any.
observations.managementAction: Management partially implemented the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: The unadjusted difference as of March 23, 2022, increased to ₱213,200.00 from previous year’s balance of ₱18,595.16.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer, Disbursing Officer and the Municipal Accountant reconciles the cashbooks and the accounting records at least quarterly to correct reconciling items in a timely manner in accordance with Section 181 (c) of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Management was able to partially implement the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: The recommendation was partially implemented by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the inventory committee/team to immediately conduct the physical inventory of all properties and submit the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property and Plant and Equipment to the auditor in accordance with Section 124 of Manual on New Government Accounting System (NGAS), Volume1.  Henceforth, require the reconciliation of records maintained by the Office of the Municipal Accountant with the Property/Equipment Card maintained by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer and effect the necessary adjustments when appropriate.
observations.managementAction: Management partially implemented the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: Management is at 80% in the inventory and reconciliation of their PPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the purchases of welfare goods and supplies and materials using the perpetual inventory method pursuant to Section  114 of NGAS Volume I Manual and the Municipal Treasurer/GSO to prepare the Summary of  Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and submit it to the Municipal Accountant which shall be used as basis in recording the issuances/expense account as required under Section 121 of the same manual.

We recommend that management provide a written explanation about the non-posting in the GPPB online portal the documents relative to the procurement of projects undertaken under the Bayanihan to Heal as One Act. Also, communicate with the GPPB if delayed posting of Notice of Award is possible.

We recommend that management provide a written explanation on the recording process of the financial assistance. Also,  we recommend that the Office of the Municipal Accountant reconcile the check issued with the distribution list from the Municipal Treasurer and set up any unreconciled amount as receivable. Forward a copy of the Liquidation Report and supporting documents to the COA Office for Audit.

Further, instruct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the journal entries made as follows

Prior Year’s Adjustment

We recommend that Management instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to reconcile the charges made to the Bayanihan Grant to reflect the proper balance of the fund at year-end and provide accurate data for decision making.

We recommend that management submit the supporting documents and photos specifying the detailed item specification and brand name of the rapid test kits purchased to prove compliance with FDA advisory.

We recommend that management require  the Municipal Accountant to capitalize the cost of major repair made on its firefighting equipment in accordance with IPSAS 17 on the Property, Plant and Equipment to reflect the accurate value of the account in the financial statement.

We recommend that Management require the Municipal Accountant to record the fund transfers to its appropriate account as required in COA Circular No. 2015-009  in order to accurately present actual balances of the accounts in the year-end financial statements and facilitate monitoring of the implementation of the  projects.
Also, instruct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the journal entries as follows:
Due From Local Government Units	₱549,423.80
Prior Years Adjustments			₱549,423.80

We recommend that Management require the Municipal Accountant to book the unrecorded assets and correct the accounting entries made to ensure the correctness of the account balances in the books at year-end.

We recommend that management relieve the Municipal Treasurer of her disbursing function.  Instead, have the checks for financial assistance  to qualified Senior Citizens and Person with Disabilities be in the name of the Disbursing Officer for proper recording and subsequent  liquidation,  and for an adequate internal control system.

We recommend that management require the GAD Focal Point System to: (i) institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-aggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation; and (ii) undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing gender issues and ensure that the programs, projects and activities are responsive to the said issue

We recommend that management closely monitor and ensure that the LDRRM disaster preparedness planned program/projects/activities are implemented so that intended purpose is attained/achieved as required Section 1 and 2 of Rule 18, Republic Act 10121.
observations.managementAction: Complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to maintain Property Cards per category of property and equipment in accordance with  Section 119 of the Manual on New Government Accounting System (NGAS), Volume1. Henceforth, require the reconciliation of records maintained by the Office of the Municipal Accountant with the Property/Equipment Card maintained by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer  and effect the necessary adjustments when appropriate. Further, require the inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory/count of its property, plant and equipment and prepare the RPCPPE.
observations.managementAction: Complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the preparation of the Monthly Report of Official Travel and the Fuel Consumption Report for each vehicle and  submit the same to the auditor.

Furthermore, everytime the vehicle/heavy equipment shall be refuelled at the refilling station, a checker/inspector should accompany and check whether the correct quantity of fuel being issued is the same as that pumped into the vehicle or heavy equipment.
observations.managementAction: Complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipality establish the Local Council for the Protection of Children and allocate 1% of its IRA for the programs. projects and activities for the protection of children and youth in accordance with the DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2002-121 and Section 15 of Republic Act 9344.
observations.managementAction: Complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant submit the year-end Financial Statements of the Municipality and its supporting schedules on time. Henceforth, submit the quarterly trial balances and financial statements within the reglementary period to ensure compliance with aforecited regulations and ensure the relevance of data for various purposes.
observations.managementAction: Complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
Instruct the Municipal Assessor, Municipal Treasurer and all other concerned to work for the immediate titling, recording or transfer of ownership of all land belonging to the LGU.

Record all acquired real properties in the books of the municipality upon securing proper and valid papers to support the documentation/titling.

Intensify the verification process and titling of the Land owing to the LGU and booked up in the LGU’s account and said lots to establish ownership and avoid the risk of court litigation from the previous owners who are interested to take back the properties.
observations.managementAction: The previous  municipal assessor already filed all the necessary documents and undertake the necessary steps for the land titling.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management increase the amount of bond of the DO or consider the use of ATM cards in the payment of salaries to its regular and elected employees to reduce the amount of cash in the hands of the DO and to safeguard government funds from possible loss or misuse in accordance with Sections 23 and 24 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to trace the transactions recorded in the Due from Other Funds and Due To Other Funds accounts and make the necessary corrections/adjustments.  Moreover, require the Municipal Treasurer to settle these accounts to allow the creditor-fund to utilize the amounts for their intended purposes, if any.
observations.managementAction: Management partially implemented the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer, Disbursing Officer and the Municipal Accountant reconciles the cashbooks and the accounting records at least quarterly to correct reconciling items in a timely manner in accordance with Section 181 (c) of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Management was able to partially implement the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the inventory committee/team to immediately conduct the physical inventory of all properties and submit the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to the auditor in accordance with Section 124 of Manual on New Government Accounting System (NGAS), Volume1.  Henceforth, require the reconciliation of records maintained by the Office of the Municipal Accountant with the Property/Equipment Card maintained by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer and effect the necessary adjustments when appropriate.
observations.managementAction: Management partially implemented the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the Municipal Agriculturist to trace the record of these assets, regularly monitor the changes of Work/Zoo Animals and Breeding Stocks to facilitate the proper valuation of these assets in order to reflect an accurate value of the account.
observations.managementAction: Management was not able to implement the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant evaluate the Receivable accounts and validate its existence and collectability. observations.managementAction: Management was not able to implement the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant trace the Due to BIR, Due to GSIS, Due to Pag-ibig and Due to PhilHealth accounts and ascertain the validity of the balances of these payables. Effect the necessary adjustments relating to the accounts after reconciliation.
observations.managementAction: Management was not able to implement the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year which data should come from the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) or Taxpayer’s index card maintained at the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and furnish the Municipal Accountant copy of the list to serve as the basis in recording the Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable accounts in the books. observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the concerned municipal officials to properly program/plan and monitor the implementation of projects to assure its completion and the intended purpose be achieved. observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to comply with the documentary requirements for repairs, and maintain records for each property. Also, require the personnel concerned to subject the damaged parts of the vehicle/equipment to pre-repair inspection before requisition for replacement is made and to the replaced parts to post repair inspection by the designated LGU Inspector to ascertain that the vehicle/equipment is in operational condition and the parts requested were really installed and to maintain a Subsidiary Ledger for repairs of each vehicle. Furthermore, require the Municipal Accountant to see to it that all claims for repairs are supported with complete documentation to ensure that government funds are expended for proper and valid expenses as required under existing regulations.
observations.managementAction: Complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the inventory committee/team to
immediately conduct the physical inventory of all properties and submit the
corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to the
auditor in accordance with Section 124 of Manual on New Government Accounting
System (NGAS), Volume1. Henceforth , require the reconciliation of records
maintained by the Office of the Municipal Accountant with the Property/Equipment
Card maintained by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer and effect the necessary
adjustments when appropriate.

We recommend that the Head of the Procuring Entity issue a Blacklisting
order for the erring contractor and forfeit the Performance security in accordance
with the GPPB Uniform guidelines for the blacklisting of manufacturers, suppliers,
distributors, contractors and consultant.

We recommend that the Municipality establish the Local Council for the
Protection of Children and allocate 1% of its IRA for the programs. projects and
activities for the protection of children and youth in accordance with the DILG
Memorandum Circular No. 2002-121 and Section 15 of Republic Act 9344.

We recommend that management require the concerned municipal officials to
properly program/plan and monitor the implementation of projects to assure its
completion and the intended purpose be achieved. In the future they have to
thoroughly evaluate the viability of these project before procurement is effected to
ensure the attainment of intended benefits and to avoid wastage of funds

We recommend the management to comply with the documentary
requirements for repairs, and maintain records for each property. Also, require the
personnel concerned to subject the damaged parts of the vehicle/equipment to pre-
repair inspection before requisition for replacement is made and to the replaced parts
to post repair inspection by the designated LGU Inspector to ascertain that the
vehicle/equipment is in operational condition and the parts requested were really
installed and to maintain a Subsidiary Ledger for repairs of each vehicle.
Furthermore, require the Municipal Accountant to see to it that all claims for repairs
are supported with complete documentation to ensure that government funds are
expended for proper and valid expenses as required under existing regulations.

We recommend that management require the preparation of the Monthly
Report of Official Travel and the Fuel Consumption Report for each vehicle and
submit the same to the auditor.

Furthermore, everytime the vehicle/heavy equipment shall be refueled at the
refilling station, a checker/inspector should accompany and check whether the
correct quantity of fuel being issued is the same as that pumped into the vehicle or
heavy equipment.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant submit the year-end Financial
Statements of the Municipality and its supporting schedules on time. Henceforth,
submit the quarterly trial balances and financial statements within the reglementary
period to ensure compliance with aforecited regulations and ensure the relevance of
data for various purposes.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant reconcile their records
regularly and continue to validate prior year’s transactions affecting the Real
Property Tax Receivable and Deferred Real Property Tax account and effect
adjustments to arrive at the correct account balance.

We recommend that management record the donated ambulance under motor vehicle account and compute the corresponding depreciation in accordance to COA Circular 2015-009 to accurately reflect the financial position of the agency at year-end.

We recommend that management ensure the liquidation of the financial assistance granted to NGOs/POs be made upon completion of the project or the intended purpose has been served in accordance with existing regulations and to refrain from extending grant to recipients with unliquidated financial assistance.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant adhere to the provisions of Section 271(d) of Republic Act 7160 to avoid the accumulation of trust funds and hamper the implementation of the barangay programmed during the year. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer should facilitate the updating and reconciling of records, thus presenting fairly the actual liability of the local government unit and actual income of the barangay and the release of the current year’s real property taxes to the barangay immediately, as well as the succeeding years’ share on said income within five(5) days after the end of each quarter to augment its meager resources and ensure its prompt utilization.

We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to transfer the interest earned on deposits of the Trust Fund to the General Fund as required under Section 122 of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual Volume I.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require all accountable officers/collectors and the Municipal Treasurer to remit all collections daily and deposit the same not later than the next banking day or where collections are minimal and daily deposit thereof becomes costly and impractical, deposit their collections as soon as collections reach P10,000.00 We further recommend that necessary sanctions shall be imposed, if necessary.

We recommend that management ensure the liquidation of the financial
assistance granted to NGOs/POs be made upon completion of the project or the
intended purpose has been served in accordance with existing regulations and to refrain
from extending grant to recipients with unliquidated financial assistance.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant
adhere to the provisions of Section 271(d) of Republic Act 7160 to avoid the
accumulation of trust funds in the municipal treasury and hamper the implementation
of the barangay projects programmed during the year. The Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer should facilitate the updating and reconciling of records, thus
presenting fairly the actual liability of the local government unit and actual income of
the barangay. Lastly facilitate the immediate release of the current year’s real property
taxes to the barangay as well as the succeeding years’ share on said income within
five(5) days after the end of each quarter to augment its meager resources and ensure its
prompt utilization.

We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer to transfer the interest earned on deposits of the Trust Fund to
the General Fund as required under Section 122 of the Government Accounting and
Auditing Manual Volume I.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require all accountable
officers/collectors and the Municipal Treasurer to remit all collections daily and deposit
the same not later than the next banking day or where collections are minimal and daily
deposit thereof becomes costly and impractical, deposit their collections as soon as
collections reach P10,000.00 We further recommend that necessary sanctions shall be
imposed, if necessary.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to record
the fund transfers to its appropriate account pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009 in
order to present the correct balances of the accounts in the year-end financial
statements. Henceforth, require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify their liability
accounts using the following Journal Entry:
Due to LGUs 3,052,780.08
Due to NGAS 2,155,520.00
Due to other Funds 897,260.08

In view of the SC’s decision on the unconstitutionality of the PDAF, we
recommend that the remaining funds shall not be released and shall be returned to the
Department of Budget and Management (DBM) so that it can be used with the other
priority programs of the government.

We recommend that the Disbursing Officer demand official receipt whenever
cash is handed upon liquidation and follows the guidelines enumerated under COA
Circular 97-002. We further recommend that management consider replacing the
liquidating officer and strengthens its internal control in cash collections and issuance
of official receipts.

We recommend that the disbursing officer and the accountant reconcile the
cashbook and the accounting records at least quarterly to correct reconciling items in a
timely manner in accordance with Section 181 (c) of the Government Accounting and
Auditing Manual, Volume I.
